phase-scope.phase-27: Foundation evidence exists for pending-registration primitives, token hashing, TTL/replay protection, email risk checks, fake-provider email delivery, and unchanged active /register behavior.
phase-scope.phase-28: Primary cutover verification proves that /register is a pending-email flow and that active KrotPN access exists only after verified email activation; full wave closure is partial until V-M-004 and V-M-022 complete gates pass.
phase-scope.phase-34: Stabilization gate must prove verified-email activation and the free MTProto owner path are linked without issuing MTProto, trial, VPN, referral, or JWT side effects before email proof.

verification-levels.module-local: Risk checks, pending-token lifecycle, activation idempotency, and redaction run without live email provider, DNS mutability, VPN host mutation, or MTProto runtime.
verification-levels.wave-level: Phase-28 must run together with V-M-002 auth, V-M-004 billing trial, V-M-005 referral, V-M-022 device provisioning, and V-M-009 frontend onboarding gates.
verification-levels.phase-level: Changing /api/v1/auth/register is allowed only when tests prove the new pending response shape and no pre-verification active access.

module-checks.check-1: Allowed email input normalizes consistently and stores exactly one pending registration record without issuing JWT, cookies, trial, VPN device, VPN client, referral, or MTProto side effects.
module-checks.check-2: Verification tokens are stored hashed or otherwise non-recoverable; plaintext tokens never persist or appear in logs.
module-checks.check-3: Temporary, disposable, configured blocked, malformed, duplicate, expired, and replayed token paths fail with stable API errors.
module-checks.check-4: Blocked-domain registration is rejected before M-040 email sending is called.
module-checks.check-5: Allowlist policy can override broad deny rules for operator-approved domains.
module-checks.check-6: Successful verification consumes the token, creates the canonical user, marks email ownership verified, and returns the same authenticated session contract used by login.
module-checks.check-7: Activation side effects are executed exactly once even if the verify endpoint is called repeatedly or a transaction is retried after partial failure.
module-checks.check-8: Verified activation plus first owner MTProto access creates exactly one MTProto assignment; pending/unverified registration creates none.

phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-1: Unverified registration returns pending/check-email and leaves User, Subscription, UserDevice, Referral, and MTProtoAssignment counts unchanged.
phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-2: Verified-email activation creates normal KrotPN access once and allows the owner MTProto endpoint to create or reuse exactly one assignment.
phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-3: Replay of the verification token cannot duplicate KrotPN onboarding side effects or MTProto assignments.
phase-34-stabilization-gates.gate-4: Phase-28 partial-local blockers in V-M-004 and V-M-022 must be resolved or explicitly recorded as a release risk before Phase-34 closure.

phase-28-cutover-gates.gate-1: /api/v1/auth/register returns a pending/check-email response and never returns access_token, refresh_token, user profile, or authenticated cookies.
phase-28-cutover-gates.gate-2: /api/v1/auth/verify-email or the chosen equivalent endpoint accepts a valid one-time token and creates active access only after email ownership is proven.
phase-28-cutover-gates.gate-3: Verified activation creates exactly one eligible trial, one primary VPN device path, and one referral linkage where applicable.
phase-28-cutover-gates.gate-4: Token replay, duplicate email, expired token, and blocked-domain paths cannot duplicate trial, referral, VPN, or future MTProto side effects.

scenarios.scenario-1: New user submits allowed email and password, receives safe check-email state, and remains unauthenticated until verification.
scenarios.scenario-2: New user verifies a valid token and receives a normal authenticated response compatible with existing login/session consumers.
scenarios.scenario-3: Temporary email domain is blocked before verification email is sent.
scenarios.scenario-4: Malformed, expired, replayed, duplicate, and already-consumed pending-registration tokens fail with safe reasons.
scenarios.scenario-5: Pending registration cannot emit JWT tokens or provision KrotPN resources.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Mask email local parts in logs and never log verification tokens, token hashes, password values, JWT values, cookies, or raw risk-provider payloads.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Failure reasons may be operator-readable but must not expose whether an unrelated account exists.

autonomous-execution-safety.reason: Phase-28 changes public auth behavior. Execution is safe only with fake email provider tests, local DB isolation, no deploy/install edits, no live email provider calls, and no host VPN mutation during automated tests.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-13: Phase-27 backend/tests/test_email_verification_guard.py passed locally, covering disposable-domain blocking before email send, allowlist override, env domain parsing, HMAC token storage, one-time consume, expiry, zero user side effects, and old active /register compatibility boundary.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-13: Phase-28 backend suite passed locally: tests/test_verified_registration.py, tests/test_email_verification_guard.py, tests/test_manual_access_policy.py, tests/test_device_api.py, tests/test_referrals.py, tests/test_telegram_auth.py, and tests/test_security.py; 44 passed.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-14: MyGRACE reviewer/refresh found full V-M-004 and V-M-022 per-module commands failing on 404 route/API binding tests, so V-M-041 is partial-local at wave level while module-local cutover tests remain green.
